name: Odanacatib
description: Odanacatib is an inhibiter of cathepsin K which was originally developed be Merck & Co as a new treatment for osteoporosis [A19388]. The drug made it to phase III trials before abandoned due to increased stroke.
toxicity: Odanacatib was found to increase the risk of stroke [A19388]. No further toxicological studies have been performed.
mechanismOfAction: Odanacatib inhibits cathepsin K, likely by binding to its active site [A19398]. Cathepsin K is a cysteine protease enzyme which is secreted by osteoclasts [A19396]. Cathepsin K is responsible for the breakdown of collagen in the bone matrix as part of bone resorption. The inhibition of this enzyme results in decreased bone resorption without affecting bone deposition resulting in increased bone mineral density. This increased bone mineral density strengthens the bone which leads to fewer fractures in osteoporosis.
pharmacodynamics: Increases bone mineral density and reduces risk of fractures in osteoporosis [A19389].

interactions.name: Fusidic Acid
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Odanacatib can be increased when it is combined with Fusidic Acid.

interactions.description: The serum concentration of Conivaptan can be increased when it is combined with Odanacatib.
